title: Tobacco Use Project
text:
Methods
Briefly discuss how the data was acquired and cleaned, and the tools you used for data exploration (further details should be provided on another page of your website).

title: Tobacco Use Project
text:
Results
Provide final, publication-ready tables and figures from your analysis, including at least one interactive graphic. Make separate subsections as needed.

Interactive Dashboard

 

Note: If a plot is not loading for a certain selection criteria, try deleting the Nicotine/Product use criteria and adding it again. This dashboard can also be found separately on this website.

title: Tobacco Use Project
text:
Conclusion and Summary
A brief recap where you describe your findings.

title: About the Data
text:
Describe the raw data in detail and every step you took to prepare it for your analysis. Describe how the data was acquired, processed, and explored. This is also where you should include any exploratory plots.
In addition to submitting a written report, you will prepare a brief (10 minute) presentation on your project. The sections in your presentation should be the same as the sections of your report. Strong emphasis should be put on visualizations in the presentation. Also, you can optionally demo your website during the presentation.
In structuring your presentation, make sure to justify to your audience why you made the decisions you did. If you tried/considered multiple methods of data analysis, show some motivation for why you made the eventual decision. All figures that you show should be sufficiently explained to the audience. You should explain why the figures that you show support your conclusions. Design your slides and speech for clarity.

title: Olivia Kaitlyn Roberts
text:
Download CV
Welcome to my website! I am a second year master’s student at the University of Michigan School of Public Health studying Computational Epidemiology and Systems Modeling with a graduate certificate in Complex Systems. My current area of research focuses on tobacco control, where I analyze trends in tobacco use using multistate transition modeling as part of the data analysis and modeling team for the Center for the Assessment of Tobacco Regulations (CAsToR).
I earned my undergraduate degree in Mathematics, with minors in Music and Interdisciplinary Sciences, from the University of South Dakota in 2024. During my time there, I did research on the intersection of mathematics and music, which continues to be an area of interest in my life. I am passionate about teaching mathematics and have experience working as a math tutor, a course assistant for a high school summer math camp, and an instructor assistant for a nursing statistics course.
Outside of academia, I am also a dedicated yoga practitioner and teacher; I have been teaching yoga since 2021 and have organized yoga festivals and workshops. I hold two 200-hour yoga teaching certifications and have experience teaching vinyasa, sculpt, and ashtanga yoga.
